title: The Absence of Signal Is Itself a Signal

runtime.expected_outcome: A 'silent' observation (wall_hit=clear) still moves the posterior, because P(clear|s) varies across tiles.

symbols.P(clear|s): varies by tile; the absence of a hit is still state-dependent

audiences.kid: You didn't hear a splash. That's a clue too: maybe nothing's nearby.
audiences.real: Absence-of-observation is not zero information. As long as P(absence | s) differs across states, it updates the posterior.
audiences.equation: q(s|clear) = q(s) P(clear|s) / Z. Non-uniform P(clear|s) -> non-trivial update.
audiences.derivation: Bayes' theorem applies to any observation, including the 'no event' one. Information is asymmetry across states; absence is no exception (Pearl 1988).

orchestrate.objective: Show that 'no observation' still carries information via a non-uniform likelihood.
orchestrate.context: frog_pond; compare posterior after obs={wall_hit=clear} vs. prior.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 2.
Pearl (1988), Probabilistic Reasoning in Intelligent Systems.
